target: SEC-8b.3 (Part 8b.3 Eclipse stack four-layer audit), Layer L3a paragraph closing clause — replaces the "two distinct dome-authored formulas, both outside the W004 cluster" framing of the monitor.py / inject_ai_layer.py pair with the more honest family-relationship framing recommended by curmudgeon SEC-8b.3.c4 hole 3.
current_text_html: monitor.py encodes ΔZ = −18.22 × coverage × KAPPA 1.672 (BOU 2017 baseline with KAPPA applied twice, producing ∼−29 nT), while inject_ai_layer.py’s PRED-001 Ebro calculation uses BOU × coverage × latitude_factor 0.81 (producing ∼−8 nT) — two distinct dome-authored formulas, both outside the W004 cluster of −17 to −21 nT (see the WIN-025 three-formula audit).
replacement_text_html: monitor.py encodes ΔZ = −18.22 × coverage × KAPPA 1.672 (BOU 2017 baseline with KAPPA applied twice, producing ∼−29 nT), while inject_ai_layer.py’s PRED-001 Ebro calculation uses BOU × coverage × latitude_factor 0.81 (producing ∼−8 nT — a BOU-family variant with a non-standard per-station weight, sitting inside the BOU per-station band of −5 to −13 nT that the dome publishes across nine stations, but not matching the dome’s own Ebro BOU prediction of −10.7 nT, which uses the published FSF of 1.029). Read fairly, that is the dome’s BOU-baseline path with an internal low-latitude correction plus a separate monitor.py formula — not two independent dome formulas outside the W004 cluster. The monitor.py track is the genuine outlier: KAPPA appears twice (once baked into the −18.22 baseline, since −10.9 × 1.672 ≡ −18.22, then again as the explicit 1.672 multiplier) without published derivation, producing a magnitude outside both published baselines and the W004 cluster of −17 to −21 nT (see the WIN-025 three-formula audit).
rationale_summary: Curmudgeon SEC-8b.3.c4 hole 3 (moderate): the "two distinct dome-authored formulas, both outside the W004 cluster" phrasing is literally true (both magnitudes are outside −17 to −21 nT) but obscures that inject_ai_layer.py is a BOU-family variant, not an independent third formula. A dome defender would correctly reply "inject_ai_layer.py is my BOU-baseline path with a latitude correction — same formula family." The reframe isolates monitor.py as the actual outlier (KAPPA applied twice, no derivation), which sharpens the critique rather than weakening it.
holes_addressed: SEC-8b.3.c4 hole 3 (moderate): inject_ai_layer.py vs monitor.py family-relationship framing
new_evidence_added: BOU per-station band −5 to −13 nT explicitly named (from raw-text/03-predictions.txt entries E001–E009)
Dome's own Ebro BOU prediction (−10.7 nT, FSF 1.029) cited as the specific point of contrast that inject_ai_layer.py does not match
Explicit arithmetic identity −10.9 × 1.672 ≡ −18.22 to justify the "KAPPA baked into the baseline" claim (was implicit in prior text)

anticipated_objections.objection: You've now let inject_ai_layer.py off the hook — calling it "BOU-family" implies it's consistent with the public BOU path, which is exactly what we should be attacking.
anticipated_objections.response: No — we acknowledge family membership while preserving the specific mismatch: the dome's own published Ebro BOU prediction is −10.7 nT (FSF 1.029), inject_ai_layer.py produces −8.4 nT (using latitude_factor 0.81), and these are not the same number for the same station. The critique is now an internal-versus-public-prediction mismatch within the BOU family rather than a fabricated "third independent formula." That is a more honest and less rebuttable framing.

anticipated_objections.objection: The latitude_factor 0.81 IS the dome's FSF equivalent for low-latitude stations; you're inventing a discrepancy.
anticipated_objections.response: The dome publishes the FSF for Ebro (latitude 40.8°N) as 1.029, not 0.81. If 0.81 is the latitude-corrected FSF for some other framing, the dome has not published the derivation or the unification. The PRED-095 derivation path in section L3a is explicitly ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF; substituting a different per-station weight from inject_ai_layer.py is the very inconsistency we are flagging.

anticipated_objections.objection: Saying KAPPA is "applied twice" is an interpretation — −18.22 is just an empirical baseline that happens to round near −10.9 × 1.672.
anticipated_objections.response: The arithmetic identity −10.9 × 1.672 ≡ −18.22 to four significant figures is what the published numbers themselves say. Either monitor.py constructed −18.22 = BOU × KAPPA and then applied KAPPA a second time as the explicit 1.672 multiplier (the most natural reading), or it independently arrived at a baseline numerically identical to BOU × KAPPA by coincidence. The defender owes either a derivation that produces −18.22 from first principles independent of KAPPA, or an explanation for the coincidence. Neither appears in monitor.py, raw-text/05-model.txt, or predictions.html.

anticipated_objections.objection: This reframe weakens the rhetorical force of "three coexisting formulas" — the original "two formulas both outside the cluster" was more memorable.
anticipated_objections.response: The next paragraph in the section ("three dome-authored formulas — BOU single-baseline, W004 per-station, and monitor.py KAPPA-squared — coexist in the repository") already counts the public BOU and W004 paths as two separate formulas and adds monitor.py as the third, NOT counting inject_ai_layer.py separately. The reframe brings the L3a paragraph into alignment with the immediately following paragraph; the section was internally inconsistent before this patch.

kernel_of_truth: The dome's BOU-baseline path IS internally coherent as a public computation: one baseline (BOU 2017, −10.9 nT), per-station coverage values, and per-station FSF values, with the per-station BOU predictions (E001–E009) all sitting in a plausible band of −5 to −13 nT depending on coverage and FSF. The dome author got that structure right; inject_ai_layer.py is a variant within that family, not a hostile third formula. The honest critique is structural — monitor.py is the formula that contradicts the public predictions page while living in the same repository — not the rhetorically convenient "every dome file disagrees with every other dome file."
why_kernel_doesnt_save_claim: Even after the reframe, the substantive concerns survive intact: (1) monitor.py contains a magnitude (−29 nT) outside every dome-published per-station prediction and both dome-published baselines, with KAPPA applied twice without derivation; (2) inject_ai_layer.py does not reproduce the dome's own Ebro BOU prediction (−10.7 nT vs −8.4 nT); (3) the W004 Pass band of −17 to −21 nT remains non-discriminating from a coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo fit on the same Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble; (4) the broader L3a non-discrimination and Fail-clause double-bind arguments are untouched by this minor framing change.
summary_for_decider: Targeted sentence-level rephrase of SEC-8b.3 L3a paragraph 3 closing clause. Replaces "two distinct dome-authored formulas, both outside the W004 cluster" framing of the monitor.py / inject_ai_layer.py pair with the family-relationship framing the curmudgeon recommended in SEC-8b.3.c4 hole 3, isolating monitor.py as the genuine outlier (KAPPA applied twice; the explicit arithmetic identity −10.9 × 1.672 ≡ −18.22 is now stated). Verification class: no new arguments, no new sub-sections, no defender-pivot — the KAPPA-twice critique was already present in the text and in PRED-095 detail_reasoning; the BOU-family acknowledgment is the new framing element. Aligns the L3a paragraph with the immediately following goalpost-scope paragraph (which already counts BOU, W004, monitor.py as the three formulas without including inject_ai_layer.py).
